title: NDBC Salinity Map
text:
Code
library(dplyr)
library(httr2)
library(htmltools)
library(knitr)
library(leaflet)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(viridisLite)

# Station list and parameters mirror ndbc_fetch_airflow_dag.py
inwater_params <- c("sea_water_practical_salinity", "sea_water_temperature")

ndbc_stations <- list(
  list(name = "bkyf1", long_name = "Buoy_Key"),
  list(name = "bnkf1", long_name = "Butternut_Kay"),
  list(name = "bobf1", long_name = "Bob_Allen_Key"),
  list(name = "bwsf1", long_name = "Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "dkkf1", long_name = "Duck_Key"),
  list(name = "gbtf1", long_name = "Garfield_Bight"),
  list(name = "hcef1", long_name = "Highway_Creek"),
  list(name = "jbyf1", long_name = "Joe_Bay"),
  list(name = "jkyf1", long_name = "Johnson_Key"),
  list(name = "lbsf1", long_name = "Little_Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "lmdf1", long_name = "Little_Madeira_Bay"),
  list(name = "lrkf1", long_name = "Little_Rabbit_Key"),
  list(name = "lsnf1", long_name = "Long_Sound"),
  list(name = "mdkf1", long_name = "Middle_Key"),
  list(name = "mukf1", long_name = "Murray_Key"),
  list(name = "pkyf1", long_name = "Peterson_Key"),
  list(name = "tbyf1", long_name = "Terrapin_Bay"),
  list(name = "thrf1", long_name = "Thursday_Point"),
  list(name = "wrbf1", long_name = "Whipray_Basin")
)

# 25-hour lookback window
end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")
start_time <- format(Sys.time() - 25 * 3600, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")

fetch_latest_salinity <- function(station_info) {
  param <- "sea_water_practical_salinity"
  url <- sprintf(
    "https://erddap.secoora.org/erddap/tabledap/gov-nps-ever-%s.csv?time%%2Clatitude%%2Clongitude%%2C%s&time%%3E=%s&time%%3C=%s",
    station_info$name,
    param,
    start_time,
    end_time
  )

  resp <- request(url) |>
    req_perform()

  df <- read_csv(resp_body_string(resp), show_col_types = FALSE)
  names(df) <- c("time", "latitude", "longitude", "salinity")
  df <- df[-1, ] |>
    mutate(
      time = as.POSIXct(time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC"),
      latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
      longitude = as.numeric(longitude),
      salinity = as.numeric(salinity)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(salinity))

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  latest <- df[which.max(df$time), ]
  tibble(
    station_id = station_info$name,
    station = station_info$long_name,
    location = sprintf(
      "%s (%s)",
      station_info$long_name,
      sub("f1$", "", station_info$name)
    ),
    time = latest$time,
    latitude = latest$latitude,
    longitude = latest$longitude,
    salinity = latest$salinity
  )
}

salinity_data <- bind_rows(lapply(ndbc_stations, fetch_latest_salinity))
report_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")
Data are pulled from SECOORA ERDDAP: using a 25-hour window ending at render time, with the most recent non-missing salinity value kept for each station.
Report generated: 2026-07-09 14:15 EDT
Query window: 2026-07-08T17:14:51Z to 2026-07-09T18:14:51Z
Code
if (nrow(salinity_data) == 0) {
  stop("No salinity data returned from ERDDAP for the current time window.")
}

salinity_data |>
  arrange(desc(time)) |>
  mutate(
    time = format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC"),
    salinity = number(salinity, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) |>
  select(Station = station, `Station ID` = station_id, Time = time, `Salinity (PSU)` = salinity) |>
  kable()

Code
salinity_palette <- colorNumeric(
  palette = c("#2166ac", "#67a9cf", "#d1e5f0", "#fddbc7", "#ef8a62", "#b2182b"),
  domain = salinity_data$salinity,
  na.color = "#999999"
)

leaflet(salinity_data) |>
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") |>
  setView(
    lng = mean(salinity_data$longitude),
    lat = mean(salinity_data$latitude),
    zoom = 9
  ) |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius = 8,
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = 1,
    color = "#333333",
    fillColor = ~salinity_palette(salinity),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    label = ~sprintf(
      "%s: %.1f PSU\n%s",
      location,
      salinity,
      format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    pal = salinity_palette,
    values = salinity_data$salinity,
    title = "Salinity (PSU)",
    opacity = 0.9
  )
Salinity is shown in practical salinity units (PSU, parts per thousand). Lower values (blue) indicate fresher water; higher values (red) indicate more marine conditions.

title: NDBC Salinity Map
text:
Interpolated salinity field
The field below uses DIVAnd (Data-Interpolating Variational Analysis) with bathymetry masking, following the same approach as sfer-mbon-oxygen: ~0.01° horizontal grid resolution (~1 km), correlation length (0.05, 0.05) degrees, and epsilon2 = 1.0. Land is masked using Natural Earth 10 m coastlines plus NOAA BlueTopo 4 m bathymetry (8 m tiles fill gaps); cells with missing bathymetry or elevation ≥ −0.2 m are treated as land. Sea cells are extended along water paths from each buoy (not across islands), then short bridges are added only through BlueTopo water cells (elevation < −0.2 m) to connect nearby stations still isolated from the main field.


Code
obs_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "salinity_obs.csv")
field_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "salinity_field.csv")
dir.create(dirname(obs_file), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

salinity_data |>
  select(longitude, latitude, salinity) |>
  write_csv(obs_file)

interp_log <- system2(
  "scripts/run_interpolate_field.sh",
  c(obs_file, field_file, "ndbc_buoys"),
  stdout = TRUE,
  stderr = TRUE
)
exit_code <- attr(interp_log, "status")
if (!is.null(exit_code) && exit_code != 0L) {
  stop(paste("Julia interpolation failed:\n", paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n")))
}
cat(paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n"), "\n")


Interpolating field from salinity_obs.csv
Loaded 19 observations for salinity
Grid size: 62 x 42 (2604 points)
Loaded BlueTopo (400x336 4 m) from ndbc_buoys_bluetopo_400x336.tif; land/unknown cells: 1014/2604 (NA and elev >= -0.2 m treated as land)
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.8013, 25.1672) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.4443, 25.2542) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.3964, 25.2893) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.7248, 25.1573) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.3723, 25.2034) within 40 steps
Bridged 1 cell(s) along water paths for 1 observation(s)
Extended sea mask up to 4 step(s) along water from each observation
Kept 6 sea component(s) with observations; removed 406 disconnected cell(s)
Wrote data/interpolated/salinity_field.csv (1194 grid cells) 


Code
salinity_field <- read_csv(field_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
cat("Interpolated field cells:", nrow(salinity_field), "\n")


Interpolated field cells: 1194 


Code
source("R/plot_ndbc_field.R")




Code
if (nrow(salinity_field) == 0) {
  stop("Interpolated salinity field is empty.")
}

plot_salinity_field(salinity_field, salinity_data)






The colored grid shows the DIVAnd-interpolated field; circle markers show the buoy observations used as input.

title: NDBC Temperature Map
text:
Code
library(dplyr)
library(httr2)
library(htmltools)
library(knitr)
library(leaflet)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(viridisLite)

# Station list and parameters mirror ndbc_fetch_airflow_dag.py
ndbc_stations <- list(
  list(name = "bkyf1", long_name = "Buoy_Key"),
  list(name = "bnkf1", long_name = "Butternut_Kay"),
  list(name = "bobf1", long_name = "Bob_Allen_Key"),
  list(name = "bwsf1", long_name = "Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "dkkf1", long_name = "Duck_Key"),
  list(name = "gbtf1", long_name = "Garfield_Bight"),
  list(name = "hcef1", long_name = "Highway_Creek"),
  list(name = "jbyf1", long_name = "Joe_Bay"),
  list(name = "jkyf1", long_name = "Johnson_Key"),
  list(name = "lbsf1", long_name = "Little_Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "lmdf1", long_name = "Little_Madeira_Bay"),
  list(name = "lrkf1", long_name = "Little_Rabbit_Key"),
  list(name = "lsnf1", long_name = "Long_Sound"),
  list(name = "mdkf1", long_name = "Middle_Key"),
  list(name = "mukf1", long_name = "Murray_Key"),
  list(name = "pkyf1", long_name = "Peterson_Key"),
  list(name = "tbyf1", long_name = "Terrapin_Bay"),
  list(name = "thrf1", long_name = "Thursday_Point"),
  list(name = "wrbf1", long_name = "Whipray_Basin")
)

# 25-hour lookback window
end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")
start_time <- format(Sys.time() - 25 * 3600, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")

fetch_latest_temperature <- function(station_info) {
  param <- "sea_water_temperature"
  url <- sprintf(
    "https://erddap.secoora.org/erddap/tabledap/gov-nps-ever-%s.csv?time%%2Clatitude%%2Clongitude%%2C%s&time%%3E=%s&time%%3C=%s",
    station_info$name,
    param,
    start_time,
    end_time
  )

  resp <- request(url) |>
    req_perform()

  df <- read_csv(resp_body_string(resp), show_col_types = FALSE)
  names(df) <- c("time", "latitude", "longitude", "temperature")
  df <- df[-1, ] |>
    mutate(
      time = as.POSIXct(time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC"),
      latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
      longitude = as.numeric(longitude),
      temperature = as.numeric(temperature)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(temperature))

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  latest <- df[which.max(df$time), ]
  tibble(
    station_id = station_info$name,
    station = station_info$long_name,
    location = sprintf(
      "%s (%s)",
      station_info$long_name,
      sub("f1$", "", station_info$name)
    ),
    time = latest$time,
    latitude = latest$latitude,
    longitude = latest$longitude,
    temperature = latest$temperature
  )
}

temperature_data <- bind_rows(lapply(ndbc_stations, fetch_latest_temperature))
report_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")
Data are pulled from SECOORA ERDDAP using a 25-hour window ending at render time, with the most recent non-missing temperature value kept for each station.
Report generated: 2026-07-09 14:13 EDT
Query window: 2026-07-08T17:13:40Z to 2026-07-09T18:13:40Z

    temperature = number(temperature, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) |>
  select(Station = station, `Station ID` = station_id, Time = time, `Temperature (°C)` = temperature) |>

temperature_palette <- colorNumeric(
  palette = c("#313695", "#74add1", "#ffffbf", "#f46d43", "#a50026"),
  domain = temperature_data$temperature,
  na.color = "#999999"
)

leaflet(temperature_data) |>
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") |>
  setView(
    lng = mean(temperature_data$longitude),
    lat = mean(temperature_data$latitude),
    zoom = 9
  ) |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius = 8,
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = 1,
    color = "#333333",
    fillColor = ~temperature_palette(temperature),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    label = ~sprintf(
      "%s: %.1f °C\n%s",
      location,
      temperature,
      format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    pal = temperature_palette,
    values = temperature_data$temperature,
    title = "Temperature (°C)",
    opacity = 0.9
  )
Temperature is shown in degrees Celsius. Cooler values (blue) and warmer values (red) reflect spatial variation across the buoy network.

title: NDBC Temperature Map
text:
Interpolated temperature field
The field below uses DIVAnd (Data-Interpolating Variational Analysis) with bathymetry masking, following the same approach as sfer-mbon-oxygen: ~0.01° horizontal grid resolution (~1 km), correlation length (0.05, 0.05) degrees, and epsilon2 = 1.0. Land is masked using Natural Earth 10 m coastlines plus NOAA BlueTopo 4 m bathymetry (8 m tiles fill gaps); cells with missing bathymetry or elevation ≥ −0.2 m are treated as land. Sea cells are extended along water paths from each buoy (not across islands), then short bridges are added only through BlueTopo water cells (elevation < −0.2 m) to connect nearby stations still isolated from the main field.


Code
obs_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "temperature_obs.csv")
field_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "temperature_field.csv")
dir.create(dirname(obs_file), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

temperature_data |>
  select(longitude, latitude, temperature) |>
  write_csv(obs_file)

interp_log <- system2(
  "scripts/run_interpolate_field.sh",
  c(obs_file, field_file, "ndbc_buoys"),
  stdout = TRUE,
  stderr = TRUE
)
exit_code <- attr(interp_log, "status")
if (!is.null(exit_code) && exit_code != 0L) {
  stop(paste("Julia interpolation failed:\n", paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n")))
}
cat(paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n"), "\n")

temperature_field <- read_csv(field_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
cat("Interpolated field cells:", nrow(temperature_field), "\n")


Interpolated field cells: 1194 


Code
source("R/plot_ndbc_field.R")




Code
if (nrow(temperature_field) == 0) {
  stop("Interpolated temperature field is empty.")
}

plot_ndbc_field(
  temperature_field,
  temperature_data,
  value_col = "temperature",
  legend_title = "Temperature (°C)",
  popup_suffix = " °C",
  palette = c("#313695", "#74add1", "#abd9e9", "#fee090", "#f46d43", "#a50026")
)






The colored field shows the DIVAnd-interpolated temperature; circle markers show the buoy observations used as input.
